tools.name: check_http
tools.description: Check HTTP status, response time, and size for a URL. Follows redirects. Returns: status=<code> time_ms=<N> size_bytes=<N>.
args: if [ "${LEATHER_DEMO_MODE:-dry}" = live ]; then curl -o /dev/null -w 'status=%{http_code} time_ms=%{time_starttransfer_ms} size_bytes=%{size_download}' -sL --max-time 15 "$1" 2>&1 || echo 'status=error time_ms=0 size_bytes=0'; else printf 'status=200 time_ms=142 size_bytes=24096  # dry-mode fixture for %s\n' "$1"; fi

tools.name: check_tls
tools.description: Check TLS certificate expiry for a hostname. Returns the notAfter date line (e.g. notAfter=Jun 15 00:00:00 2026 GMT).
args: if [ "${LEATHER_DEMO_MODE:-dry}" = live ]; then echo | openssl s_client -connect "$1":443 -servername "$1" 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -enddate 2>/dev/null || echo 'notAfter=unknown (TLS check failed)'; else echo 'notAfter=Sep 30 23:59:59 2026 GMT  # dry-mode fixture'; fi

tools.name: read_package_json
tools.description: Read and return the contents of package.json from a project directory.
args: cat "$1/package.json" 2>/dev/null || echo 'ERROR: package.json not found at '$1

tools.name: npm_outdated
tools.description: List outdated npm packages. Returns table of package name, current, wanted, and latest versions. Requires node_modules (run npm install first).
args:
if [ "${LEATHER_DEMO_MODE:-dry}" = live ]; then cd "$1" && npm outdated 2>&1 || true; else printf 'Package          Current  Wanted   Latest  Location
react            18.2.0   18.3.1   19.0.0  node_modules/react
lodash           4.17.20  4.17.21  4.17.21 node_modules/lodash
# dry-mode fixture for %s
' "$1"; fi

tools.name: npm_audit
tools.description: Run npm security audit and return a summary of vulnerabilities by severity.
args: if [ "${LEATHER_DEMO_MODE:-dry}" = live ]; then cd "$1" && npm audit --summary 2>&1 || true; else printf '0 vulnerabilities (0 low, 0 moderate, 0 high, 0 critical)  # dry-mode fixture\n'; fi

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: Get the last N git commits with file change counts. repo_path must be inside a git repository.
args: cd "$1" && git log --oneline --stat -"$2" 2>/dev/null || echo 'ERROR: not a git repository or git not installed'

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the content of a file by its full or relative path. Returns up to 4000 bytes.
args: cat "$1" 2>/dev/null | head -c 4000 || echo 'ERROR: file not found: '$1
